questions.q: Iltutmish is regarded as the true consolidator of which medieval Indian state?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish gave firm administrative shape to the Delhi Sultanate, earning recognition as its real consolidator.

questions.q: Iltutmish was the third Sultan of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish belonged to the Mamluk or Slave dynasty, the first dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: Which silver coin did Iltutmish introduce?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish introduced the silver tanka, which became a standard coin of the Sultanate currency system.

questions.q: Iltutmish completed the construction of which famous Delhi monument?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish completed the Qutb Minar that had been begun by Qutb-ud-din Aibak.

questions.q: In which century did Iltutmish reign?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish ruled from 1211 to 1236 CE, in the early 13th century CE.

questions.q: Iltutmish ruled the Delhi Sultanate from which capital city?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish made Delhi the firm seat of the Sultanate, shifting its centre of gravity there from Lahore.

questions.q: Whose slave and son-in-law was Iltutmish before becoming Sultan?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish was a slave and son-in-law of Qutb-ud-din Aibak, succeeding to the throne after him.

questions.q: Iltutmish named which of his children as his chosen successor?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish nominated his daughter Razia as successor, judging her abler than his sons.

questions.q: Iltutmish was the Sultan who shifted the Sultanate's main capital to Delhi from which city?
questions.explanation: Under Iltutmish, Delhi replaced Lahore as the principal capital of the Sultanate.

questions.q: Iltutmish was of which ethnic origin?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish was a Turk of the Ilbari tribe of Central Asia, sold into slavery as a youth.

questions.q: Iltutmish organised an elite corps of forty leading Turkish nobles known as the:
questions.explanation: Iltutmish created the Chihalgani or 'Forty', a powerful group of loyal Turkish nobles who later dominated court politics.

questions.q: Iltutmish secured formal recognition of his sovereignty by obtaining investiture from whom in 1229?
questions.explanation: In 1229 the Abbasid Caliph sent Iltutmish a letter of investiture, legitimising his rule over the Sultanate.

questions.q: Iltutmish introduced which administrative system of land grants in lieu of cash salaries?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish systematised the iqta arrangement, assigning revenue of territories to officers in return for service.

questions.q: Iltutmish wisely avoided direct conflict with which Central Asian conqueror who reached the Indus pursuing a fugitive prince?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish prudently refused asylum to the fleeing Jalaluddin Mangabarni, avoiding war with Genghis Khan around 1221.

questions.q: Besides the tanka, Iltutmish also issued a copper coin known as the:
questions.explanation: Iltutmish issued the copper jital alongside the silver tanka, establishing a bimetallic Sultanate currency.

questions.q: Iltutmish defeated which rival Ghurid slave general who held Sindh and Multan?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish defeated Nasiruddin Qabacha to bring Sindh and Multan under Delhi's control, eliminating a key rival.

questions.q: Roughly how long did Iltutmish reign over the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish reigned for about twenty-five years, from 1211 to 1236, a long and stabilising rule.

questions.q: Iltutmish suppressed a rival claimant named Yildiz at which historic battlefield in 1215-16?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish defeated Tajuddin Yildiz near Tarain around 1215-16, removing a contender for Ghurid succession.

questions.q: Iltutmish's tomb, an early example of Indo-Islamic architecture, stands in which complex?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish's tomb, built around 1235, lies in the Qutb complex and is among the earliest surviving Sultanate tombs.

questions.q: Iltutmish recovered control over which important Rajput-held fort, among others, during his campaigns?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish campaigned to reassert Sultanate authority over forts such as Ranthambore and Gwalior in the north.

questions.q: Iltutmish completed and added storeys to the Qutb Minar, which had originally been begun by:
questions.explanation: Aibak laid the first storey of the Qutb Minar; Iltutmish added further storeys to complete the early tower.

questions.q: Which large artificial reservoir in Delhi is traditionally attributed to Iltutmish?
options: The Hauz Khas of Firuz Shah
questions.explanation: The Hauz-i-Shamsi tank near Mehrauli is credited to Iltutmish (Shams-ud-din), supplying water to early Delhi.

questions.q: Iltutmish's full regnal name was:
questions.explanation: Iltutmish took the title Shams-ud-din, meaning 'sun of the faith', reflected in the name of the Hauz-i-Shamsi.

questions.q: Iltutmish was originally enslaved and sold after being betrayed by which relatives, according to chroniclers?
questions.explanation: Chronicles relate that Iltutmish's envious brothers sold him into slavery, from which he rose to the throne.

questions.q: Which contemporary historian, author of the Tabaqat-i-Nasiri, chronicled Iltutmish's reign?
questions.explanation: Minhaj-us-Siraj's Tabaqat-i-Nasiri is a principal contemporary source for the reign of Iltutmish and his successors.

questions.q: The threat from which rising power to the northwest shaped Iltutmish's cautious frontier policy?
questions.explanation: The Mongol advance under Genghis Khan loomed over Iltutmish's northwest, prompting his careful avoidance of provocation.

questions.q: Iltutmish appointed governors over which eastern region, reasserting Delhi's claim after rebellions there?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish moved to reassert authority over Bengal, which had drifted toward independence under local commanders.

questions.q: Iltutmish's choice of his daughter over his sons reflected his judgement of the sons as:
questions.explanation: Iltutmish found his surviving sons devoted to pleasure and incapable, so he named the capable Razia his heir.

questions.q: Iltutmish died in which year, after returning from a campaign?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish died in 1236 CE, following which a turbulent succession eventually brought his daughter Razia to power.

questions.q: The Chihalgani nobles created by Iltutmish later became a problem chiefly because they:
options: Grew powerful and meddled in the succession
questions.explanation: After Iltutmish, the Forty nobles became overmighty, repeatedly interfering in the choice of sultans until Balban broke them.
